chapter_title.ar: باب الرَّجُلِ يُعْطِي مِنْ زَكَاةٍ مَنْ يَظُنُّ أَنَّهُ مُعْسِرٌ ثُمَّ يَجِدُهُ مُوسِراً
chapter_title.en: Chapter 27 – The man gives from the Zakāt to the one whom he thinks is financially straitened, then he finds him to be affluent

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن بسنديه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/81)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن بسنديه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/81)
text: رَجُلٍ عَجَّلَ زَكَاةَ مَالِهِ ثُمَّ أَيْسَرَ الْمُعْطَى قَبْلَ رَأْسِ السَّنَةِ قَالَ يُعِيدُ الْمُعْطِي الزَّكَاةَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father and Muhbammad Bin Ismail, from Al Fazl Bin Shazaan, altogether from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Al Ahowl,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding a man who hastened with the Zakāt of his wealth, then the recipient became affluent before the end of the year. Heasws said: ‘The giver would repeat the Zakāt’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il has narrated from al-Fadl ibn Shadhan from all from ibn abu ‘Umayr from al-Ahwal who has said the following: “About a man who pays his Zakat before due time then the recipient becomes affluent before due time (end of the year), abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Zakat payer must pay again.’”
